vulnerabilities.title: OS Command Injection in Locutus
vulnerabilities.description: php/exec/escapeshellarg in Locutus PHP through 2.0.11 allows an attacker to achieve code execution.

vulnerabilities.title: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption in locutus
vulnerabilities.description: The package locutus before 2.0.15 is vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via the gopher_parsedir function.

vulnerabilities.title: Prototype Pollution in locutus
vulnerabilities.description: All versions of package locutus prior to version 2.0.12 are vulnerable to Prototype Pollution via the php.strings.parse_str function.

vulnerabilities.title: locutus call_user_func_array vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) due to Code Injection
vulnerabilities.description:
### Details

A Remote Code Execution (RCE) flaw was discovered in the `locutus` project (v2.0.39), specifically within the `call_user_func_array` function implementation. The vulnerability allows an attacker to inject arbitrary JavaScript code into the application's runtime environment. This issue stems from an insecure implementation of the `call_user_func_array` function (and its wrapper `call_user_func`), which fails to properly validate all components of a callback array before passing them

vulnerabilities.title: locutus is vulnerable to Prototype Pollution
vulnerabilities.description:
### Summary
A Prototype Pollution vulnerability exists in the the npm package locutus (>2.0.12). Despite a previous fix that attempted to mitigate Prototype Pollution by checking whether user input contained a forbidden key, it is still possible to pollute Object.prototype via a crafted input using String.prototype. This issue was fixed in version 2.0.39.

### Details
The vulnerability resides in line 77 to 79 of https://github.com/locutusjs/locutus/blob/main/src/php/strings/parse_str.js where i
